summary: France's transition is steered by ANSSI, which has set out a phased approach to the move to post-quantum cryptography, with standalone post-quantum deployments envisaged only after the initial hybrid phases.
migrationTimeline:
2030 | High-risk use cases migrated
2035 | Full migration of all systems complete
provenance: Standalone not before 2030: backend brief reference line
Hybrid Required: ANSSI doctrine for products handling long-lived secrets - REVIEW
dominantProcess omitted: unattested in captured text (verifier)
dominantProcess Sovereign restored: well-established and design-endorsed, provisional for review

EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one. Secondary Sovereign reflects the national doctrine.
Model 2026-06-14 (EU / contextualiser, High): ANSSI mandates hybridisation and national certification over NIST algorithms; no national algorithm. Sovereign secondary dropped.
processParticipation: NIS Cooperation Group | co-authored the EU coordinated PQC roadmap
